content.text: Starting with Phase 1 — the new TestHarness schema.

content.content:
1	"""
2	Test harness schema — what init.sh must produce for the reviewer.
3	
4	The test harness tells the reviewer exactly how to verify features end-to-end:
5	- Where the running app is (base URL, port)
6	- How to authenticate (curl command, storageState, API token)
7	- What seed data exists (test users, sample records)
8	- What commands to run for e2e verification
9	- What health checks confirm the environment is alive
10	
11	This is NOT a test manifest (that defines unit/integration test commands).
12	This IS a runtime verification spec: "here's a running system, here's how to poke it."
13	"""
14	
15	from enum import Enum
16	from pathlib import Path
17	from typing import Optional
18	
19	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
20	
21	
22	class AuthMethod(str, Enum):
23	    """How the reviewer can authenticate against the running app."""
24	    NONE = "none"                    # No auth needed (public endpoints)
25	    BEARER_TOKEN = "bearer_token"    # Static token from env var or init output
26	    COOKIE = "cookie"                # Login via curl, use cookie
27	    STORAGE_STATE = "storage_state"  # Playwright storageState.json
28	    BASIC_AUTH = "basic_auth"        # HTTP Basic Auth
29	    API_KEY = "api_key"              # API key in header
30	
31	
32	class AuthConfig(BaseModel):
33	    """How to authenticate for e2e testing."""
34	    method: AuthMethod = AuthMethod.NONE
35	    # For bearer_token / api_key: env var name containing the token (NEVER the value)
36	    token_env_var: str = ""
37	    # For cookie / bearer_token: a curl command that returns a token/session
38	    # e.g. "curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"email\":\"test@test.com\",\"password\":\"test\"}' | jq -r '.token'"
39	    login_command: str = ""
40	    # For storage_state: path to the Playwright storageState.json
41	    storage_state_path: str = ""
42	    # For basic_auth: env var names (NEVER values)
43	    username_env_var: str = ""
44	    password_env_var: str = ""
45	
46	
47	class SeedUser(BaseModel):
48	    """A test user seeded by init.sh."""
49	    role: str = Field(..., description="e.g. admin, user, viewer")
50	    email: str = ""
51	    # NEVER the actual password — either an env var name or a known test default
52	    password_env_var: str = ""
53	    password_default: str = Field("", description="Only for test-only defaults like 'test123'")
54	    notes: str = ""
55	
56	
57	class SeedData(BaseModel):
58	    """What data init.sh seeded into the system."""
59	    users: list[SeedUser] = Field(default_factory=list)
60	    description: str = Field("", description="What other data was seeded (records, fixtures, etc.)")
61	    seed_command: str = Field("", description="Command to re-run seeding if needed")
62	
63	
64	class HealthCheck(BaseModel):
65	    """A command to verify a service is alive."""
66	    name: str
67	    command: str = Field(..., description="Bash command that exits 0 when healthy")
68	    timeout_seconds: int = 30
69	
70	
71	class E2eCommand(BaseModel):
72	    """A command the reviewer can run for e2e verification."""
73	    name: str = Field(..., description="What this verifies")
74	    command: str = Field(..., description="Bash command to run")
75	    expected: str = Field("", description="What success looks like (exit 0, contains string, etc.)")
76	
77	
78	class E2eFrameworkConfig(BaseModel):
79	    """Configuration for a specific e2e framework (Playwright/Puppeteer/Cypress)."""
80	    framework: str = Field(..., description="playwright, puppeteer, cypress, or none")
81	    config_file: str = ""
82	    run_command: str = Field("", description="e.g. 'npx playwright test --project=chromium'")
83	    # If the framework has its own auth setup (Playwright globalSetup, etc.)
84	    has_global_setup: bool = False
85	    global_setup_file: str = ""
86	
87	
88	class TestHarness(BaseModel):
89	    """
90	    The test harness: everything the reviewer needs to verify features end-to-end.
91	
92	    Produced by init.sh (or init phase), consumed by the reviewer.
93	    Lives at .dirigent/test-harness.json.
94	    """
95	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
96	
97	    # Where is the app?
98	    base_url: str = Field(..., description="e.g. http://localhost:3000")
99	    port: int = 3000
100	
101	    # How to authenticate?
102	    auth: AuthConfig = Field(default_factory=AuthConfig)
103	
104	    # What seed data exists?
105	    seed: SeedData = Field(default_factory=SeedData)
106	
107	    # Is everything running?
108	    health_checks: list[HealthCheck] = Field(default_factory=list)
109	
110	    # What e2e framework is configured?
111	    e2e_framework: E2eFrameworkConfig = Field(
112	        default_factory=lambda: E2eFrameworkConfig(framework="none")
113	    )
114	
115	    # Commands the reviewer can run to verify the feature works
116	    # These are the payoff — concrete, runnable verification steps
117	    verification_commands: list[E2eCommand] = Field(default_factory=list)
118	
119	    # Services that init started (for context)
120	    services: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="e.g. ['postgres', 'redis']")
121	
122	    # ── Testability assessment ──
123	
124	    testability_score: int = Field(
125	        0, ge=0, le=10,
126	        description="0-10 score: how well can the reviewer verify this project end-to-end?"
127	    )
128	    testability_rationale: str = Field(
129	        "",
130	        description="Why this score? What contributes to or detracts from testability?"
131	    )
132	    testability_description: str = Field(
133	        "",
134	        description="Plain-text description of the test setup: what works, what's available, "
135	                    "how the reviewer can verify features"
136	    )
137	    testability_gaps: list[str] = Field(
138	        default_factory=list,
139	        description="What's missing or broken — each gap is an actionable improvement"
140	    )
141	
142	    # Overall status
143	    status: str = Field("ready", description="ready, partial, failed")
144	    notes: str = ""
145	    infra_tier: str = "7_none"  # InfraTier value set by InfraDetector
146	
147	    # Citation map: field path → file:line where the value was found
148	    sources: dict[str, str] = Field(
149	        default_factory=dict,
150	        alias="_sources",
151	        description="Citation map: field path → file:line where the value was found"
152	    )
153	
154	    def save(self, path: Path):
155	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
156	        path.write_text(self.model_dump_json(indent=2, by_alias=True), encoding="utf-8")
157	
158	    @classmethod
159	    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Optional["TestHarness"]:
160	        if not path.exists():
161	            return None
162	        try:
163	            return cls.model_validate_json(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
164	        except Exception:
165	            return None
166	
167	    @staticmethod
168	    def json_template() -> str:
169	        return """{
170	  "base_url": "http://localhost:3000",
171	  "port": 3000,
172	  "auth": {
173	    "method": "bearer_token",
174	    "token_env_var": "",
175	    "login_command": "curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"email\":\"test@test.com\",\"password\":\"test123\"}' | jq -r '.token'",
176	    "storage_state_path": "",
177	    "username_env_var": "",
178	    "password_env_var": ""
179	  },
180	  "seed": {
181	    "users": [
182	      {
183	        "role": "admin",
184	        "email": "admin@test.com",
185	        "password_env_var": "",
186	        "password_default": "test123",
187	        "notes": "Full access, seeded by init.sh"
188	      }
189	    ],
190	    "description": "10 sample products, 3 orders",
191	    "seed_command": "npm run db:seed"
192	  },
193	  "health_checks": [
194	    {

file.content:
"""
Test harness schema — what init.sh must produce for the reviewer.

The test harness tells the reviewer exactly how to verify features end-to-end:
- Where the running app is (base URL, port)
- How to authenticate (curl command, storageState, API token)
- What seed data exists (test users, sample records)
- What commands to run for e2e verification
- What health checks confirm the environment is alive

This is NOT a test manifest (that defines unit/integration test commands).
This IS a runtime verification spec: "here's a running system, here's how to poke it."
"""

from enum import Enum
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field


class AuthMethod(str, Enum):
    """How the reviewer can authenticate against the running app."""
    NONE = "none"                    # No auth needed (public endpoints)
    BEARER_TOKEN = "bearer_token"    # Static token from env var or init output
    COOKIE = "cookie"                # Login via curl, use cookie
    STORAGE_STATE = "storage_state"  # Playwright storageState.json
    BASIC_AUTH = "basic_auth"        # HTTP Basic Auth
    API_KEY = "api_key"              # API key in header


class AuthConfig(BaseModel):
    """How to authenticate for e2e testing."""
    method: AuthMethod = AuthMethod.NONE
    # For bearer_token / api_key: env var name containing the token (NEVER the value)
    token_env_var: str = ""
    # For cookie / bearer_token: a curl command that returns a token/session
    # e.g. "curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"email\":\"test@test.com\",\"password\":\"test\"}' | jq -r '.token'"
    login_command: str = ""
    # For storage_state: path to the Playwright storageState.json
    storage_state_path: str = ""
    # For basic_auth: env var names (NEVER values)
    username_env_var: str = ""
    password_env_var: str = ""


class SeedUser(BaseModel):
    """A test user seeded by init.sh."""
    role: str = Field(..., description="e.g. admin, user, viewer")
    email: str = ""
    # NEVER the actual password — either an env var name or a known test default
    password_env_var: str = ""
    password_default: str = Field("", description="Only for test-only defaults like 'test123'")
    notes: str = ""


class SeedData(BaseModel):
    """What data init.sh seeded into the system."""
    users: list[SeedUser] = Field(default_factory=list)
    description: str = Field("", description="What other data was seeded (records, fixtures, etc.)")
    seed_command: str = Field("", description="Command to re-run seeding if needed")


class HealthCheck(BaseModel):
    """A command to verify a service is alive."""
    name: str
    command: str = Field(..., description="Bash command that exits 0 when healthy")
    timeout_seconds: int = 30


class E2eCommand(BaseModel):
    """A command the reviewer can run for e2e verification."""
    name: str = Field(..., description="What this verifies")
    command: str = Field(..., description="Bash command to run")
    expected: str = Field("", description="What success looks like (exit 0, contains string, etc.)")


class E2eFrameworkConfig(BaseModel):
    """Configuration for a specific e2e framework (Playwright/Puppeteer/Cypress)."""
    framework: str = Field(..., description="playwright, puppeteer, cypress, or none")
    config_file: str = ""
    run_command: str = Field("", description="e.g. 'npx playwright test --project=chromium'")
    # If the framework has its own auth setup (Playwright globalSetup, etc.)
    has_global_setup: bool = False
    global_setup_file: str = ""


class TestHarness(BaseModel):
    """
    The test harness: everything the reviewer needs to verify features end-to-end.

    Produced by init.sh (or init phase), consumed by the reviewer.
    Lives at .dirigent/test-harness.json.
    """
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)

    # Where is the app?
    base_url: str = Field(..., description="e.g. http://localhost:3000")
    port: int = 3000

    # How to authenticate?
    auth: AuthConfig = Field(default_factory=AuthConfig)

    # What seed data exists?
    seed: SeedData = Field(default_factory=SeedData)

    # Is everything running?
    health_checks: list[HealthCheck] = Field(default_factory=list)

    # What e2e framework is configured?
    e2e_framework: E2eFrameworkConfig = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: E2eFrameworkConfig(framework="none")
    )

    # Commands the reviewer can run to verify the feature works
    # These are the payoff — concrete, runnable verification steps
    verification_commands: list[E2eCommand] = Field(default_factory=list)

    # Services that init started (for context)
    services: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="e.g. ['postgres', 'redis']")

    # ── Testability assessment ──

    testability_score: int = Field(
        0, ge=0, le=10,
        description="0-10 score: how well can the reviewer verify this project end-to-end?"
    )
    testability_rationale: str = Field(
        "",
        description="Why this score? What contributes to or detracts from testability?"
    )
    testability_description: str = Field(
        "",
        description="Plain-text description of the test setup: what works, what's available, "
                    "how the reviewer can verify features"
    )
    testability_gaps: list[str] = Field(
        default_factory=list,
        description="What's missing or broken — each gap is an actionable improvement"
    )

    # Overall status
    status: str = Field("ready", description="ready, partial, failed")
    notes: str = ""
    infra_tier: str = "7_none"  # InfraTier value set by InfraDetector

    # Citation map: field path → file:line where the value was found
    sources: dict[str, str] = Field(
        default_factory=dict,
        alias="_sources",
        description="Citation map: field path → file:line where the value was found"
    )

    def save(self, path: Path):
        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        path.write_text(self.model_dump_json(indent=2, by_alias=True), encoding="utf-8")

    @classmethod
    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Optional["TestHarness"]:
        if not path.exists():
            return None
        try:
            return cls.model_validate_json(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except Exception:
            return None

    @staticmethod
    def json_template() -> str:
        return """{
  "base_url": "http://localhost:3000",
  "port": 3000,
  "auth": {
    "method": "bearer_token",
    "token_env_var": "",
    "login_command": "curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"email\":\"test@test.com\",\"password\":\"test123\"}' | jq -r '.token'",
    "storage_state_path": "",
    "username_env_var": "",
    "password_env_var": ""
  },
  "seed": {
    "users": [
      {
        "role": "admin",
        "email": "admin@test.com",
        "password_env_var": "",
        "password_default": "test123",
        "notes": "Full access, seeded by init.sh"
      }
    ],
    "description": "10 sample products, 3 orders",
    "seed_command": "npm run db:seed"
  },
  "health_checks": [
    {
      "name": "App server",
      "command": "curl -sf http://localhost:3000/api/health",
      "timeout_seconds": 30
    },
    {
      "name": "Database",
      "command": "pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432",
      "timeout_seconds": 10
    }
  ],
  "e2e_framework": {
    "framework": "playwright",
    "config_file": "playwright.config.ts",
    "run_command": "npx playwright test --project=chromium",
    "has_global_setup": true,
    "global_setup_file": "e2e/global-setup.ts"
  },
  "verification_commands": [
    {
      "name": "API health",
      "command": "curl -sf http://localhost:3000/api/health",
      "expected": "exit 0, returns JSON with status ok"
    },
    {
      "name": "Auth flow",
      "command": "curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"email\":\"admin@test.com\",\"password\":\"test123\"}' -w '\\n%{http_code}'",
      "expected": "HTTP 200, response contains token"
    },
    {
      "name": "E2e test suite",
      "command": "npx playwright test --project=chromium",
      "expected": "exit 0, all tests pass"
    }
  ],
  "services": ["postgres", "redis"],
  "testability_score": 7,
  "testability_rationale": "Playwright configured with auth setup and seed data. Health checks pass. Missing: no API contract tests, no visual regression tests.",
  "testability_description": "Dev server runs on localhost:3000 with Postgres. Auth via bearer token from login endpoint. Test user seeded. Playwright e2e suite available. Reviewer can run curl commands for API verification and npx playwright test for UI flows.",
  "testability_gaps": [
    "No API contract/schema tests (e.g. OpenAPI validation)",
    "No visual regression testing configured",
    "Seed data is minimal — only 1 user, no edge-case data",
    "No load/performance test baseline"
  ],
  "status": "ready",
  "notes": ""
}"""

    def summary_for_reviewer(self) -> str:
        """Compact summary for the reviewer's prompt context."""
        lines = [
            f"Base URL: {self.base_url}",
            f"Auth: {self.auth.method.value}",
        ]
        if self.auth.login_command:
            lines.append(f"Login: {self.auth.login_command}")
        if self.auth.storage_state_path:
            lines.append(f"Storage state: {self.auth.storage_state_path}")
        if self.seed.users:
            for u in self.seed.users:
                pw = f"${u.password_env_var}" if u.password_env_var else u.password_default
                lines.append(f"Test user ({u.role}): {u.email} / {pw}")
        if self.health_checks:
            lines.append("Health checks:")
            for h in self.health_checks:
                lines.append(f"  - {h.name}: {h.command}")
        if self.verification_commands:
            lines.append("Verification commands:")
            for v in self.verification_commands:
                lines.append(f"  - {v.name}: {v.command}")
        if self.e2e_framework.framework != "none":
            lines.append(f"E2e: {self.e2e_framework.run_command}")
        if self.testability_score:
            lines.append(f"Testability: {self.testability_score}/10 — {self.testability_rationale}")
        if self.testability_gaps:
            lines.append("Gaps:")
            for g in self.testability_gaps:
                lines.append(f"  - {g}")
        return "\n".join(lines)

except Exception as e:
            logger.debug(f"Failed to extract env diff: {e}")

    def wait_for_readiness(self, harness: TestHarness) -> bool:
        """Wait for health checks in the test harness to pass."""
        if not harness.health_checks:
            return True

        logger.info(f"Waiting for {len(harness.health_checks)} health checks...")
        all_passed = True

        for check in harness.health_checks:
            timeout = min(check.timeout_seconds, self.READINESS_TIMEOUT)
            deadline = time.time() + timeout
            passed = False

            while time.time() < deadline:
                try:
                    proc = subprocess.run(
                        ["bash", "-c", check.command],
                        cwd=self.repo_path,
                        capture_output=True,
                        text=True,
                        timeout=10,
                    )
                    if proc.returncode == 0:
                        logger.info(f"  Health check passed: {check.name}")
                        passed = True
                        break
                except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, Exception):
                    pass
                time.sleep(2)

            if not passed:
                logger.warning(f"  Health check failed: {check.name} (timeout {timeout}s)")
                all_passed = False

        return all_passed

    # ══════════════════════════════════════════
    # MAIN ENTRY POINT
    # ══════════════════════════════════════════

    def run(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete init phase.

        Flow:
        1. Discover and run init script (if exists)
        2. If init script produced test-harness.json → validate and use it
        3. If not → invoke /dirigent:run-init skill to inspect the repo
           and build a best-effort harness
        4. Wait for health checks in the harness to pass
        5. Harness is available for planner, executor, and reviewer

        Returns True always (non-blocking — best effort).
        """
        logger.info("Starting init phase...")

        # NEW: run InfraDetector before anything else
        detector = InfraDetector(self.repo_path, dirigent_dir=self.dirigent_dir)
        ctx = detector.detect()
        logger.info(f"Infra tier: {ctx.tier.value} | confidence: {ctx.confidence}")

        script = self.discover_init_script()
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"

        # Step 1: Run init script if it exists
        if script:
            logger.info(f"Running init script: {script}")
            init_result = self.run_init_script(script)

            if not init_result["success"]:
                logger.warning("Init script failed (continuing — non-blocking)")
        else:
            logger.info("No init script found (.outbid/init.sh or init.sh)")

        # Step 2: Check if the script produced the harness
        harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        # Step 3: If no harness yet, have the skill build one
        if harness is None and self.runner:
            logger.info("No test-harness.json — invoking /dirigent:run-init to build one")
            success, _, stderr = self.runner._run_claude(
                "Run /dirigent:run-init", timeout=300,
            )
            if success:
                harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        if harness is None:
            logger.warning("No test harness produced — reviewer will only do static analysis")

        # Step 3b: Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        arch_path = self.repo_path / "ARCHITECTURE.md"
        if not arch_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No ARCHITECTURE.md found — generating one")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-architecture", timeout=600)
        elif arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("ARCHITECTURE.md exists — skipping generation")

        # Step 3c: Generate CONVENTIONS.md if no .opencode skills and no existing file
        has_opencode = (self.repo_path / ".opencode" / "skills").is_dir()
        conv_path = self.repo_path / "CONVENTIONS.md"
        if has_opencode:
            logger.info(".opencode/skills/ detected — skipping CONVENTIONS.md (bridge handles this)")
        elif not conv_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No .opencode skills or CONVENTIONS.md — generating conventions")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-conventions", timeout=600)
        elif conv_path.exists():
            logger.info("CONVENTIONS.md exists — skipping generation")

        if harness is None:
            return True

        # Step 4: Wait for health checks
        logger.info(f"Test harness: {harness.base_url}, auth={harness.auth.method.value}, "
                    f"{len(harness.verification_commands)} verification commands")

        if harness.health_checks:
            ready = self.wait_for_readiness(harness)
            if ready:
                logger.info("All health checks passed — environment is ready")
            else:
                harness.status = "partial"
                harness.notes = "Some health checks failed"
                harness.save(harness_path)
                logger.warning("Some health checks failed — harness marked as partial")

        return True

def run_tests(self) -> bool:
        """Run build and test commands from the test harness. Returns True if passed or no harness."""
        harness = TestHarness.load(self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json")
        if not harness or not harness.commands:
            logger.info("No test harness or commands, skipping test step")
            return True

        all_passed = True

        # Run build, test, and e2e commands in order
        for key in ("build", "test", "e2e"):
            if key in harness.commands:
                cmd = harness.commands[key]
                logger.info(f"Running {key}: {cmd.command}")
                timeout = 600 if key == "e2e" else 120
                if not self._run_verification_cmd(cmd.command, key, timeout=timeout):
                    all_passed = False

        logger.info("All verification commands passed") if all_passed else logger.error("Verification failed — blocking ship")

        # Notify portal that testing is complete
        from outbid_dirigent.dirigent import get_portal_reporter
        reporter = get_portal_reporter()
        if reporter and hasattr(reporter, "testing_complete"):
            reporter.testing_complete(str(self.repo_path))

        return all_passed

file.content:
start = datetime.now()
        proc = None

        try:
            script_path.chmod(0o755)

            # Run the init script. Use a subshell that sources the script
            # so exported env vars are captured in a sidecar file.
            env_capture = self.dirigent_dir / "init-exports.env"
            wrapper = (
                f'set -e; source "{script_path}" 2>&1; '
                f'env > "{env_capture}"'
            )

            proc = subprocess.run(
                ["bash", "-c", wrapper],
                cwd=self.repo_path,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=self.INIT_TIMEOUT,
            )

            result["output"] = proc.stdout[:10000]
            result["error"] = proc.stderr[:5000]
            result["success"] = proc.returncode == 0

            if proc.returncode != 0:
                logger.warning(f"Init script exited with code {proc.returncode}")

        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            result["error"] = f"Init script timed out after {self.INIT_TIMEOUT}s"
            logger.error(result["error"])
        except Exception as e:
            result["error"] = str(e)
            logger.error(f"Init script error: {e}")

        result["duration_seconds"] = (datetime.now() - start).total_seconds()

        # Check if the script produced the harness itself
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"
        if harness_path.exists():
            harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)
            if harness:
                result["produced_harness"] = True
                logger.info("Init script produced test-harness.json directly")

        # Save raw output for the skill to analyze if needed
        log_file = self.dirigent_dir / "init-output.log"
        log_file.write_text(
            f"=== Init Script: {script_path} ===\n"
            f"=== Exit Code: {proc.returncode if proc else 'N/A'} ===\n\n"
            f"--- STDOUT ---\n{result['output']}\n\n"
            f"--- STDERR ---\n{result['error']}\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )

        # Save exported environment (from the init script's perspective)
        if env_capture.exists():
            self._extract_env_diff(env_capture)

def run(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete init phase.

        Flow:
        1. Discover and run init script (if exists)
        2. If init script produced test-harness.json → validate and use it
        3. If not → invoke /dirigent:run-init skill to inspect the repo
           and build a best-effort harness
        4. Wait for health checks in the harness to pass
        5. Harness is available for planner, executor, and reviewer

        Returns True always (non-blocking — best effort).
        """
        logger.info("Starting init phase...")

        # NEW: run InfraDetector before anything else
        detector = InfraDetector(self.repo_path, dirigent_dir=self.dirigent_dir)
        ctx = detector.detect()
        logger.info(f"Infra tier: {ctx.tier.value} | confidence: {ctx.confidence}")

        script = self.discover_init_script()
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"

        # Step 1: Run init script if it exists
        if script:
            logger.info(f"Running init script: {script}")
            init_result = self.run_init_script(script)

            if not init_result["success"]:
                logger.warning("Init script failed (continuing — non-blocking)")
        else:
            logger.info("No init script found (.outbid/init.sh or init.sh)")

        # Step 2: Check if the script produced the harness
        harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        # Step 3: If no harness yet, have the skill build one
        if harness is None and self.runner:
            logger.info("No test-harness.json — invoking /dirigent:run-init to build one")
            success, _, stderr = self.runner._run_claude(
                "Run /dirigent:run-init", timeout=300,
            )
            if success:
                harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        if harness is None:
            logger.warning("No test harness produced — reviewer will only do static analysis")

        # Step 3b: Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        arch_path = self.repo_path / "ARCHITECTURE.md"
        if not arch_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No ARCHITECTURE.md found — generating one")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-architecture", timeout=600)
        elif arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("ARCHITECTURE.md exists — skipping generation")

        # Step 3c: Generate CONVENTIONS.md if no .opencode skills and no existing file
        has_opencode = (self.repo_path / ".opencode" / "skills").is_dir()
        conv_path = self.repo_path / "CONVENTIONS.md"
        if has_opencode:
            logger.info(".opencode/skills/ detected — skipping CONVENTIONS.md (bridge handles this)")
        elif not conv_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No .opencode skills or CONVENTIONS.md — generating conventions")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-conventions", timeout=600)
        elif conv_path.exists():
            logger.info("CONVENTIONS.md exists — skipping generation")

        if harness is None:
            return True

        # Step 4: Wait for health checks
        logger.info(f"Test harness: {harness.base_url}, auth={harness.auth.method.value}, "
                    f"{len(harness.verification_commands)} verification commands")

        if harness.health_checks:
            ready = self.wait_for_readiness(harness)
            if ready:
                logger.info("All health checks passed — environment is ready")
            else:
                harness.status = "partial"
                harness.notes = "Some health checks failed"
                harness.save(harness_path)
                logger.warning("Some health checks failed — harness marked as partial")

        return True

def run(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete init phase.

        Flow:
        1. Discover and run init script (if exists)
        2. If init script produced test-harness.json → validate and use it
        3. If not → invoke /dirigent:run-init skill to inspect the repo
           and build a best-effort harness
        4. Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        5. Harness is available for planner, executor, and reviewer

        Returns True always (non-blocking — best effort).
        """
        logger.info("Starting init phase...")

        script = self.discover_init_script()
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"

        # Step 1: Run init script if it exists
        if script:
            logger.info(f"Running init script: {script}")
            init_result = self.run_init_script(script)

            if not init_result["success"]:
                logger.warning("Init script failed (continuing — non-blocking)")
        else:
            logger.info("No init script found (.outbid/init.sh or init.sh)")

toolUseResult.oldString:
def run(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete init phase.

        Flow:
        1. Discover and run init script (if exists)
        2. If init script produced test-harness.json → validate and use it
        3. If not → invoke /dirigent:run-init skill to inspect the repo
           and build a best-effort harness
        4. Wait for health checks in the harness to pass
        5. Harness is available for planner, executor, and reviewer

        Returns True always (non-blocking — best effort).
        """
        logger.info("Starting init phase...")

        # NEW: run InfraDetector before anything else
        detector = InfraDetector(self.repo_path, dirigent_dir=self.dirigent_dir)
        ctx = detector.detect()
        logger.info(f"Infra tier: {ctx.tier.value} | confidence: {ctx.confidence}")

        script = self.discover_init_script()
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"

        # Step 1: Run init script if it exists
        if script:
            logger.info(f"Running init script: {script}")
            init_result = self.run_init_script(script)

            if not init_result["success"]:
                logger.warning("Init script failed (continuing — non-blocking)")
        else:
            logger.info("No init script found (.outbid/init.sh or init.sh)")

        # Step 2: Check if the script produced the harness
        harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        # Step 3: If no harness yet, have the skill build one
        if harness is None and self.runner:
            logger.info("No test-harness.json — invoking /dirigent:run-init to build one")
            success, _, stderr = self.runner._run_claude(
                "Run /dirigent:run-init", timeout=300,
            )
            if success:
                harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        if harness is None:
            logger.warning("No test harness produced — reviewer will only do static analysis")

        # Step 3b: Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        arch_path = self.repo_path / "ARCHITECTURE.md"
        if not arch_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No ARCHITECTURE.md found — generating one")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-architecture", timeout=600)
        elif arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("ARCHITECTURE.md exists — skipping generation")

        # Step 3c: Generate CONVENTIONS.md if no .opencode skills and no existing file
        has_opencode = (self.repo_path / ".opencode" / "skills").is_dir()
        conv_path = self.repo_path / "CONVENTIONS.md"
        if has_opencode:
            logger.info(".opencode/skills/ detected — skipping CONVENTIONS.md (bridge handles this)")
        elif not conv_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No .opencode skills or CONVENTIONS.md — generating conventions")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-conventions", timeout=600)
        elif conv_path.exists():
            logger.info("CONVENTIONS.md exists — skipping generation")

        if harness is None:
            return True

        # Step 4: Wait for health checks
        logger.info(f"Test harness: {harness.base_url}, auth={harness.auth.method.value}, "
                    f"{len(harness.verification_commands)} verification commands")

        if harness.health_checks:
            ready = self.wait_for_readiness(harness)
            if ready:
                logger.info("All health checks passed — environment is ready")
            else:
                harness.status = "partial"
                harness.notes = "Some health checks failed"
                harness.save(harness_path)
                logger.warning("Some health checks failed — harness marked as partial")

        return True

from outbid_dirigent.test_harness_schema import TestHarness, CommandSpec, PortalConfig, EnvVar
import json, tempfile
from pathlib import Path

# Create with new schema
h = TestHarness(
    commands={
        'build': CommandSpec(command='npm run build', explanation='TypeScript compilation'),
        'test': CommandSpec(command='npm test', explanation='Vitest unit tests'),
        'dev': CommandSpec(command='npm run dev', explanation='Next.js dev server'),
    },
    env_vars={
        'DATABASE_URL': EnvVar(source='doppler', required=True),
        'NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL': EnvVar(source='env', required=False, default='http://localhost:3000'),
    },
    portal=PortalConfig(start_command='npm run dev', port=3000, url_after_start='/dashboard'),
    notes='No e2e framework configured yet',
    sources={'commands.build': 'package.json:8', 'commands.test': 'package.json:9'},
)

# Save
p = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.json'))
h.save(p)

# Verify JSON structure
raw = json.loads(p.read_text())
assert 'commands' in raw
assert 'build' in raw['commands']
assert raw['commands']['build']['command'] == 'npm run build'
assert '_sources' in raw
assert raw['portal']['port'] == 3000

# Load back
h2 = TestHarness.load(p)
assert h2 is not None
assert 'build' in h2.commands
assert h2.commands['build'].command == 'npm run build'
assert h2.portal.port == 3000
assert h2.sources == {'commands.build': 'package.json:8', 'commands.test': 'package.json:9'}

# Test summary_for_prompt
summary = h2.summary_for_prompt()
assert 'npm run build' in summary
assert 'DATABASE_URL' in summary

p.unlink()
print('All round-trip checks passed')
print()
print('Summary output:')
print(summary)
"

content.text:
All good. Jetzt zu deiner Frage:

**"What input is the Claude building the test harness getting?"**

Aktuell: Der `/dirigent:run-init` Skill wird via Claude Code CLI aufgerufen (`_run_claude("Run /dirigent:run-init")`). Der Agent hat Zugriff auf den vollen Repo-Kontext — er kann alle Dateien lesen. Aber er bekommt keinen strukturierten Input, er muss selbst rausfinden wo package.json ist, welche Scripts es gibt, etc.

Dein Vorschlag ist richtig: **Bevor `messages.parse()` den Harness generiert, sollte ein Claude Code Agent die relevanten Dateien lesen und ein ephemeres `repo-context.md` (oder besser: einen strukturierten String) als Input sammeln.** Das wäre:

1. Claude Code Agent liest: `package.json` scripts, `pyproject.toml`, `.env.example`, `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`, `playwright.config.*`, `vite.config.*`, `next.config.*`
2. Gibt das als strukturierten Text zurück
3. `messages.parse(output_format=TestHarness)` bekommt diesen Text als User-Message

Alternativ: Wir machen Schritt 1 deterministisch in Python (kein LLM nötig — just file reads) und geben das dem `messages.parse()` Call.

Was ist dir lieber — deterministisches Python-Gathering oder Claude Code Agent?

def run(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete init phase.

        Flow:
        1. Discover and run init script (if exists)
        2. If init script produced test-harness.json → validate and use it
        3. If not → invoke /dirigent:run-init skill to inspect the repo
           and build a best-effort harness
        4. Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        5. Harness is available for planner, executor, and reviewer

        Returns True always (non-blocking — best effort).
        """
        logger.info("Starting init phase...")

        script = self.discover_init_script()
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"

        # Step 1: Run init script if it exists
        if script:
            logger.info(f"Running init script: {script}")
            init_result = self.run_init_script(script)

            if not init_result["success"]:
                logger.warning("Init script failed (continuing — non-blocking)")
        else:
            logger.info("No init script found (.outbid/init.sh or init.sh)")

        # Step 2: Check if the script produced the harness
        harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        # Step 3: If no harness yet, have the skill build one
        if harness is None and self.runner:
            logger.info("No test-harness.json — invoking /dirigent:run-init to build one")
            success, _, stderr = self.runner._run_claude(
                "Run /dirigent:run-init", timeout=300,
            )
            if success:
                harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        if harness is None:
            logger.warning("No test harness produced — reviewer will only do static analysis")
        else:
            cmds = ", ".join(harness.commands.keys())
            logger.info(f"Test harness: commands=[{cmds}]")

        # Step 4: Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        arch_path = self.repo_path / "ARCHITECTURE.md"
        if not arch_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No ARCHITECTURE.md found — generating one")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-architecture", timeout=600)
        elif arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("ARCHITECTURE.md exists — skipping generation")

        return True

file.content:
## Token budget

Aim for 30-50% of source token count. Drop prose. Keep behavior.
"""


def compact_spec(
    spec_content: str,
    model: str = "claude-haiku-4-5",
    dirigent_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
) -> Optional[CompactSpec]:
    """Compact a markdown spec into a structured CompactSpec via one LLM call.

    Args:
        spec_content: Full markdown spec text.
        model: Model to use (haiku for speed/cost).
        dirigent_dir: Where to save SPEC.compact.json. If None, no file is written.

    Returns:
        CompactSpec on success, None on API error or refusal.
    """
    logger = get_logger()

    user_prompt = f"<spec>\n{spec_content}\n</spec>\n\nCompact this spec per the rules."

    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic()
        start = datetime.now()

        response = client.messages.parse(
            model=model,
            max_tokens=8000,
            system=COMPACTOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
            output_format=CompactSpec,
        )

        duration_ms = int((datetime.now() - start).total_seconds() * 1000)
        compact = response.parsed_output

        if compact is None:
            logger.error("Spec compactor: model refused to produce a compact spec")
            return None

        usage = response.usage
        logger.api_usage(
            component="spec_compactor",
            model=model,
            input_tokens=usage.input_tokens,

"""
Init phase — bootstraps dev environment before planning.

The init phase has two jobs:
1. Run .outbid/init.sh or init.sh to start services, seed data, set up auth
2. Produce .dirigent/test-harness.json — a structured specification that tells
   the reviewer exactly how to verify features end-to-end

The init script is expected to:
- Start required services (databases, queues, etc.)
- Start the dev server (or leave instructions for starting it)
- Seed test data (users, sample records)
- Set up e2e auth (create storageState, export tokens, etc.)
- Write .dirigent/test-harness.json OR print enough info for the
  /dirigent:run-init skill to construct it

If no init script exists, the skill still inspects the repo to build
a best-effort test harness from config files and package.json.
"""

import subprocess
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

from loguru import logger

from outbid_dirigent.infra_schema import InfraContext, InfraTier, ServiceGap, SeedInfo
from outbid_dirigent.test_harness_schema import TestHarness


class InfraDetector:
    """Detects and provisions test infrastructure in tier priority order."""

    KNOWN_DEVBOX_SERVICE_PACKAGES = ["postgresql", "redis", "mysql", "mongodb", "rabbitmq"]

def run(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete init phase.

        Flow:
        1. Discover and run init script (if exists)
        2. If init script produced test-harness.json → use it
        3. Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        4. Generate test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md via structured output

        Returns True always (non-blocking — best effort).
        """
        logger.info("Starting init phase...")

        script = self.discover_init_script()
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"

        # Step 1: Run init script if it exists
        if script:
            logger.info(f"Running init script: {script}")
            init_result = self.run_init_script(script)

            if not init_result["success"]:
                logger.warning("Init script failed (continuing — non-blocking)")
        else:
            logger.info("No init script found (.outbid/init.sh or init.sh)")

        # Step 2: Check if the script produced the harness
        harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        # Step 3: Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        arch_path = self.repo_path / "ARCHITECTURE.md"
        if not arch_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No ARCHITECTURE.md found — generating one")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-architecture", timeout=600)
        elif arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("ARCHITECTURE.md exists — skipping generation")

        # Step 4: Generate test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md
        if harness is None and arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("Generating test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md...")
            harness = generate_harness_from_architecture(arch_path, harness_path)

        if harness is None:
            logger.warning("No test harness produced — reviewer will only do static analysis")
        else:
            cmds = ", ".join(harness.commands.keys())
            logger.info(f"Test harness: commands=[{cmds}]")

        return True


HARNESS_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
You extract a structured test harness from an ARCHITECTURE.md file.

The harness contains ONLY deterministic, runnable commands found in the architecture doc.
Do NOT invent commands — only extract what is documented.

## Rules
- commands.build: the build command from the Development Workflow or Testing section
- commands.test: the test command (unit tests)
- commands.e2e: the e2e test command (if documented)
- commands.seed: the seed/data command (if documented)
- commands.dev: the dev server command
- Omit command keys that aren't documented
- env_vars: extract from the Configuration section
- portal: extract dev server command and port from Development Workflow
- _sources: for each value, cite the ARCHITECTURE.md section where you found it
- notes: anything important that doesn't fit the schema
"""


def generate_harness_from_architecture(
    arch_path: Path,
    harness_path: Path,
    model: str = "claude-haiku-4-5",
) -> Optional[TestHarness]:
    """Generate test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md via structured output.

    Reads the architecture doc and extracts commands, env vars, and portal config
    into a strict TestHarness schema. The LLM fills fields but cannot invent new ones.
    """
    dlogger = get_logger()

    arch_content = arch_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if not arch_content.strip():
        logger.warning("ARCHITECTURE.md is empty — cannot generate harness")
        return None

    user_prompt = (
        f"<architecture>\n{arch_content}\n</architecture>\n\n"
        f"Extract the test harness from this architecture document."
    )

    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic()
        start = datetime.now()

        response = client.messages.parse(
            model=model,
            max_tokens=4000,
            system=HARNESS_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
            output_format=TestHarness,
        )

        duration_ms = int((datetime.now() - start).total_seconds() * 1000)
        harness = response.parsed_output

        if harness is None:
            logger.error("Harness generation: model refused to produce output")
            return None

        usage = response.usage
        dlogger.api_usage(
            component="harness_generator",
            model=model,
            input_tokens=usage.input_tokens,
            output_tokens=usage.output_tokens,
            cache_read_tokens=getattr(usage, "cache_read_input_tokens", 0) or 0,
            cache_write_tokens=getattr(usage, "cache_creation_input_tokens", 0) or 0,
            cost_cents=int((usage.input_tokens * 1 + usage.output_tokens * 5) / 10000),
            operation="generate_harness",
            duration_ms=duration_ms,
        )

        harness.save(harness_path)
        logger.info(f"Test harness generated from ARCHITECTURE.md ({duration_ms}ms)")
        return harness

    except anthropic.APIError as e:
        logger.error(f"Harness generation API error: {e}")
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Harness generation error: {e}")
        return None

        harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        if harness is None and self.runner:
            logger.info("No test-harness.json — invoking /dirigent:run-init to build one")
            success, _, stderr = self.runner._run_claude(
                "Run /dirigent:run-init", timeout=300,
            )
            if success:
                harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        if harness is None:
            logger.warning("No test harness produced — reviewer will only do static analysis")
        else:
            cmds = ", ".join(harness.commands.keys())
            logger.info(f"Test harness: commands=[{cmds}]")

        # Step 4: Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        arch_path = self.repo_path / "ARCHITECTURE.md"
        if not arch_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No ARCHITECTURE.md found — generating one")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-architecture", timeout=600)
        elif arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("ARCHITECTURE.md exists — skipping generation")

        return True
toolUseResult.newString:
def run(self) -> bool:
        """Run the complete init phase.

        Flow:
        1. Discover and run init script (if exists)
        2. If init script produced test-harness.json → use it
        3. Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        4. Generate test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md via structured output

        Returns True always (non-blocking — best effort).
        """
        logger.info("Starting init phase...")

        script = self.discover_init_script()
        harness_path = self.dirigent_dir / "test-harness.json"

        # Step 1: Run init script if it exists
        if script:
            logger.info(f"Running init script: {script}")
            init_result = self.run_init_script(script)

            if not init_result["success"]:
                logger.warning("Init script failed (continuing — non-blocking)")
        else:
            logger.info("No init script found (.outbid/init.sh or init.sh)")

        # Step 2: Check if the script produced the harness
        harness = TestHarness.load(harness_path)

        # Step 3: Generate ARCHITECTURE.md if it doesn't exist
        arch_path = self.repo_path / "ARCHITECTURE.md"
        if not arch_path.exists() and self.runner:
            logger.info("No ARCHITECTURE.md found — generating one")
            self.runner._run_claude("Run /dirigent:generate-architecture", timeout=600)
        elif arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("ARCHITECTURE.md exists — skipping generation")

        # Step 4: Generate test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md
        if harness is None and arch_path.exists():
            logger.info("Generating test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md...")
            harness = generate_harness_from_architecture(arch_path, harness_path)

        if harness is None:
            logger.warning("No test harness produced — reviewer will only do static analysis")
        else:
            cmds = ", ".join(harness.commands.keys())
            logger.info(f"Test harness: commands=[{cmds}]")

        return True


HARNESS_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
You extract a structured test harness from an ARCHITECTURE.md file.

The harness contains ONLY deterministic, runnable commands found in the architecture doc.
Do NOT invent commands — only extract what is documented.

## Rules
- commands.build: the build command from the Development Workflow or Testing section
- commands.test: the test command (unit tests)
- commands.e2e: the e2e test command (if documented)
- commands.seed: the seed/data command (if documented)
- commands.dev: the dev server command
- Omit command keys that aren't documented
- env_vars: extract from the Configuration section
- portal: extract dev server command and port from Development Workflow
- _sources: for each value, cite the ARCHITECTURE.md section where you found it
- notes: anything important that doesn't fit the schema
"""


def generate_harness_from_architecture(
    arch_path: Path,
    harness_path: Path,
    model: str = "claude-haiku-4-5",
) -> Optional[TestHarness]:
    """Generate test-harness.json from ARCHITECTURE.md via structured output.

    Reads the architecture doc and extracts commands, env vars, and portal config
    into a strict TestHarness schema. The LLM fills fields but cannot invent new ones.
    """
    dlogger = get_logger()

    arch_content = arch_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if not arch_content.strip():
        logger.warning("ARCHITECTURE.md is empty — cannot generate harness")
        return None

    user_prompt = (
        f"<architecture>\n{arch_content}\n</architecture>\n\n"
        f"Extract the test harness from this architecture document."
    )

    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic()
        start = datetime.now()

        response = client.messages.parse(
            model=model,
            max_tokens=4000,
            system=HARNESS_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
            output_format=TestHarness,
        )

        duration_ms = int((datetime.now() - start).total_seconds() * 1000)
        harness = response.parsed_output

        if harness is None:
            logger.error("Harness generation: model refused to produce output")
            return None

        usage = response.usage
        dlogger.api_usage(
            component="harness_generator",
            model=model,
            input_tokens=usage.input_tokens,
            output_tokens=usage.output_tokens,
            cache_read_tokens=getattr(usage, "cache_read_input_tokens", 0) or 0,
            cache_write_tokens=getattr(usage, "cache_creation_input_tokens", 0) or 0,
            cost_cents=int((usage.input_tokens * 1 + usage.output_tokens * 5) / 10000),
            operation="generate_harness",
            duration_ms=duration_ms,
        )

        harness.save(harness_path)
        logger.info(f"Test harness generated from ARCHITECTURE.md ({duration_ms}ms)")
        return harness

    except anthropic.APIError as e:
        logger.error(f"Harness generation API error: {e}")
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Harness generation error: {e}")
        return None

import tempfile, json
from pathlib import Path
from outbid_dirigent.init_phase import generate_harness_from_architecture

# Create a sample ARCHITECTURE.md
arch_content = '''# MyApp — Architecture

<system-overview>
> A Next.js SaaS dashboard for managing customer data
</system-overview>

<testing-verification>
## Testing & Verification

### Build
npm run build                [source: package.json:8]

### Test Suite
npm test                     [source: package.json:9]
Vitest unit tests covering auth, API routes, and data models.

### E2E Tests
npx playwright test          [source: package.json:11]
Requires running dev server and seed data.

### Dev Server
npm run dev                  [source: package.json:6]
Port: 3000                   [source: .env.example:1]

### How to Verify Manually
1. Start dev server: npm run dev
2. Open http://localhost:3000/login
3. Login with test@example.com (password in TEST_PASSWORD env var)
</testing-verification>

<tech-stack>
## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Source |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Language | TypeScript 5.x | package.json:3 |
| Framework | Next.js 14 | package.json:5 |
| Database | PostgreSQL via Prisma | prisma/schema.prisma:1 |
| Auth | NextAuth.js | src/auth.config.ts:1 |
| Testing | Vitest + Playwright | package.json:12 |
</tech-stack>

<configuration>
## Configuration

| Variable | Purpose | Required | Source |
|----------|---------|----------|--------|
| DATABASE_URL | PostgreSQL connection | Yes | src/lib/db.ts:5 |
| NEXTAUTH_SECRET | Session encryption | Yes | src/auth.config.ts:12 |
| TEST_PASSWORD | Test user password | No | .env.test:3 |
</configuration>

<development-workflow>
## Development Workflow

\`\`\`bash
# Setup
npm install
npx prisma migrate dev

# Seed
npm run db:seed

# Run
npm run dev

# Test
npm test
\`\`\`
</development-workflow>
'''

# Write to temp file
arch_path = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.md'))
arch_path.write_text(arch_content)

harness_path = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.json'))

# Generate!
harness = generate_harness_from_architecture(arch_path, harness_path)

if harness is None:
    print('FAILED: harness is None')
else:
    print('SUCCESS!')
    print()
    print('Commands:')
    for key, cmd in harness.commands.items():
        print(f'  {key}: {cmd.command}')
        print(f'    → {cmd.explanation}')
    print()
    print('Env vars:')
    for name, var in harness.env_vars.items():
        print(f'  {name}: source={var.source}, required={var.required}')
    print()
    print('Portal:')
    print(f'  start: {harness.portal.start_command}')
    print(f'  port: {harness.portal.port}')
    print(f'  url: {harness.portal.url_after_start}')
    if harness.portal.demo_login.email:
        print(f'  demo: {harness.portal.demo_login.email}')
    print()
    print('Sources:')
    for k, v in harness.sources.items():
        print(f'  {k}: {v}')
    print()
    print('Notes:', harness.notes or '(none)')
    print()
    print('--- Raw JSON ---')
    print(json.dumps(json.loads(harness_path.read_text()), indent=2)[:2000])

# Cleanup
arch_path.unlink()
harness_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
" 2>&1

from outbid_dirigent.init_phase import generate_harness_from_architecture

arch_content = '''# MyApp — Architecture

<system-overview>
> A Next.js SaaS dashboard for managing customer data
</system-overview>

<testing-verification>
## Testing & Verification

### Build
npm run build                [source: package.json:8]

### Test Suite
rtk npm test                     [source: package.json:9]
Vitest unit tests covering auth, API routes, and data models.

### E2E Tests
npx playwright test          [source: package.json:11]
Requires running dev server and seed data.

### Dev Server
npm run dev                  [source: package.json:6]
Port: 3000                   [source: .env.example:1]

### How to Verify Manually
1. Start dev server: npm run dev
2. Open http://localhost:3000/login
3. Login with test@example.com (password in TEST_PASSWORD env var)
</testing-verification>

<configuration>
## Configuration

| Variable | Purpose | Required | Source |
|----------|---------|----------|--------|
| DATABASE_URL | PostgreSQL connection | Yes | src/lib/db.ts:5 |
| NEXTAUTH_SECRET | Session encryption | Yes | src/auth.config.ts:12 |
| TEST_PASSWORD | Test user password | No | .env.test:3 |
</configuration>

<development-workflow>
## Development Workflow

\`\`\`bash
# Setup
npm install
npx prisma migrate dev

# Seed
npm run db:seed

# Run
npm run dev

# Test
rtk npm test
\`\`\`
</development-workflow>
'''

arch_path = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.md'))
arch_path.write_text(arch_content)
harness_path = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.json'))

harness = generate_harness_from_architecture(arch_path, harness_path)

if harness is None:
    print('FAILED: harness is None')
else:
    print('SUCCESS!')
    print()
    for key, cmd in harness.commands.items():
        print(f'  {key}: {cmd.command} — {cmd.explanation[:60]}')
    print()
    for name, var in harness.env_vars.items():
        print(f'  {name}: {var.source}, required={var.required}')
    print()
    print(f'Portal: {harness.portal.start_command} → :{harness.portal.port}{harness.portal.url_after_start}')
    if harness.portal.demo_login.email:
        print(f'Demo: {harness.portal.demo_login.email}')
    print()
    print(json.dumps(json.loads(harness_path.read_text()), indent=2))

arch_path.unlink()
harness_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
" 2>&1",
      "timeout": 60000,
      "description": "Test harness generation from ARCHITECTURE.md"
    }
  }
}

{Where config lives, what env vars are required, and how config flows into the app.}

| Variable | Purpose | Required | Source |
|----------|---------|----------|--------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | PostgreSQL connection string | Yes | `src/lib/db.ts:5` |
| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` | Session encryption key | Yes | `src/auth.config.ts:12` |

## External Dependencies

{Services this system talks to. For each: what, why, and what happens if it's down.}

| Service | Purpose | Failure Mode |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| PostgreSQL | Primary data store | App down |
| Redis | Session cache, rate limiting | Degraded (falls back to DB) |
| Stripe | Payment processing | Payments fail, app continues |

content.text: Now update the greenfield-scaffold skill to write into ARCHITECTURE.md sections instead of separate files:

content.content:
1	---
2	name: greenfield-scaffold
3	description: For greenfield projects, propose a test setup and architecture best practices before planning. Produces ${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/testing-strategy.md and ${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/architecture-decisions.md consumed by the planner.
4	context: fork
5	agent: infra-architect
6	---
7	
8	# Greenfield Scaffold
9	
10	You analyze a greenfield project and produce two artifacts that guide every downstream task:
11	1. **Testing strategy** — what to test, how, with what tools
12	2. **Architecture decisions** — patterns, module structure, conventions to establish
13	
14	These artifacts become the project's engineering DNA. Every task the planner creates will reference them. Every coder instance will follow them. Get them right and the entire feature ships clean. Get them wrong and every task fights the architecture.
15	
16	## When This Runs
17	
18	After init (test harness exists), before planning. You have:
19	- `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/SPEC.md` — what's being built
20	- `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/ANALYSIS.json` — repo structure, language, framework
21	- `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/test-harness.json` — available infra (services, auth, seed data)
22	- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — system overview (if generated during init)
23	- The live repo — read whatever you need
24	
25	## Step 1: Assess What Exists
26	
27	Before proposing anything, understand what's already in place.
28	
29	```bash
30	# What test infrastructure exists?
31	ls jest.config* vitest.config* pytest.ini pyproject.toml conftest.py .rspec Gemfile 2>/dev/null
32	ls -d test/ tests/ spec/ __tests__/ e2e/ cypress/ playwright/ 2>/dev/null
33	
34	# What test runner is configured?
35	grep -r "jest\|vitest\|pytest\|rspec\|mocha\|playwright\|cypress" package.json pyproject.toml Gemfile 2>/dev/null
36	
37	# What patterns are already established?
38	ls test/**/*.test.* tests/**/*.py spec/**/*_spec.rb 2>/dev/null | head -10
39	
40	# What CI exists?
41	ls .github/workflows/*.yml .gitlab-ci.yml Jenkinsfile .circleci/config.yml 2>/dev/null
42	```
43	
44	If `.brv/context-tree/` exists and `brv` CLI is available, query for existing architectural decisions:
45	```bash
46	brv query "What architecture patterns, testing strategies, and design decisions exist?"
47	```
48	Incorporate any relevant BRV knowledge into your strategy — don't propose patterns that contradict curated decisions.
49	
50	Read `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/ANALYSIS.json` for detected language, framework, and test infrastructure.
51	Read `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/test-harness.json` for available services and testability score.
52	Read `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/SPEC.md` for feature requirements.
53	
54	## Step 2: Decide Test Strategy
55	
56	Choose the right testing approach based on what you found. Not every project needs every layer.
57	
58	### Decision Framework
59	
60	| Signal | Strategy |
61	|--------|----------|
62	| API/backend project, services available | **Integration-heavy**: real DB, real HTTP, minimal mocking |
63	| Frontend SPA, no backend in scope | **Component-heavy**: component tests + visual snapshots |
64	| Full-stack feature, e2e framework detected | **E2E-anchored**: e2e happy paths + unit for logic |
65	| Library/utility, no services | **Unit-heavy**: pure function tests, edge cases |
66	| CLI tool | **Snapshot + integration**: golden file tests, subprocess tests |
67	
68	### Test Layers (pick what applies)
69	
70	**Layer 1 — Static analysis** (always)
71	- Type checking (TypeScript strict, mypy, pyright)
72	- Linting (ESLint, ruff, rubocop)
73	- Cost: zero runtime, catches ~30% of bugs
74	
75	**Layer 2 — Unit tests** (when there's logic)
76	- Pure functions, transformations, validators, business rules
77	- No I/O, no network, no database
78	- Framework: match what's already in the project, or:
79	  - TypeScript → Vitest (fast, ESM-native)
80	  - Python → pytest (fixtures, parametrize)
81	  - Ruby → Minitest or RSpec (match existing)
82	  - Go → stdlib testing
83	  - Rust → built-in #[test]
84	
85	**Layer 3 — Integration tests** (when there's a database or API)
86	- Real database (not SQLite-in-memory unless prod uses SQLite)
87	- Real HTTP requests to running server
88	- Factory/fixture pattern for test data
89	- Framework: same as unit, but with test DB setup/teardown
90	
91	**Layer 4 — E2E tests** (when there's a UI or multi-service flow)
92	- Only for critical user journeys (3-5 scenarios max)
93	- Framework: Playwright (preferred), Cypress (if already in project)
94	- Test against running dev server
95	- Store auth state to avoid login in every test
96	
97	### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
98	
99	- **Mocking what you own**: Don't mock your own database or services. Mock external APIs only.
100	- **Testing implementation**: Test behavior ("user sees error message"), not implementation ("function throws at line 42").
101	- **100% coverage goals**: Coverage is a byproduct, not a target. 80% meaningful coverage > 100% with `/* istanbul ignore */`.
102	- **Slow test suites**: If tests take > 30s locally, developers won't run them. Parallelize or split.
103	- **Snapshot abuse**: Snapshots for large objects/HTML rot fast. Use them for small, stable structures only.
104	
105	## Step 3: Decide Architecture Patterns
106	
107	For greenfield features, establish patterns BEFORE coding starts. The first file sets the precedent.
108	
109	### Decision Framework
110	
111	Read the spec and decide which patterns apply. Only propose patterns the feature actually needs.
112	
113	**API Design:**
114	
115	| Signal | Pattern |
116	|--------|---------|
117	| CRUD-heavy, resource-oriented | REST with consistent URL scheme (`/api/v1/{resource}`) |
118	| Complex queries, multiple consumers | GraphQL with schema-first design |
119	| Internal service, simple contract | RPC-style (tRPC, gRPC, or plain functions) |
120	
121	**Data Access:**
122	
123	| Signal | Pattern |
124	|--------|---------|
125	| Complex queries, multiple models | Repository pattern (DB logic in dedicated classes) |
126	| Simple CRUD, one model per route | Direct ORM in route handlers (simpler) |
127	| Multiple data sources | Service layer between routes and data |
128	
129	**Error Handling:**
130	
131	| Signal | Pattern |
132	|--------|---------|
133	| API with consumers | Typed error responses with error codes (`{ error: "NOT_FOUND", message: "..." }`) |
134	| Internal tool | Exceptions with context (no catch-all) |
135	| Multi-step pipeline | Result type (`Ok/Err` or `{success, data, error}`) |
136	
137	**State Management (frontend):**
138	
139	| Signal | Pattern |
140	|--------|---------|
141	| Server state (API data) | React Query / SWR / TanStack Query (cache + revalidation) |
142	| Client state (UI state) | Local component state or context (no Redux for small apps) |
143	| Complex forms | React Hook Form or Formik |
144	
145	**File Organization:**
146	
147	| Signal | Pattern |
148	|--------|---------|
149	| Feature-scoped work | Feature folders (`features/auth/`, `features/dashboard/`) |
150	| Shared utilities, libraries | Layer folders (`lib/`, `utils/`, `services/`) |
151	| Monorepo with multiple apps | Package-per-app with shared packages |
152	
153	### Principles (always apply)
154	
155	1. **Explicit dependencies** — pass them in, don't import globals. Makes testing trivial.
156	2. **One file, one concept** — a file should be about one thing. If you need a comment header to separate sections, split the file.
157	3. **Errors at boundaries** — validate at the edge (API handler, CLI parser). Internal code trusts the types.
158	4. **Configuration as data** — env vars → typed config object at startup. No `process.env.X` scattered through business logic.
159	5. **Naming is documentation** — `getUserById(id)` needs no JSDoc. `process(data)` needs a rewrite.
160	
161	## Step 4: Write the Artifacts
162	
163	### Artifact 1: `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/testing-strategy.md`
164	
165	```markdown
166	# Testing Strategy
167	
168	## Layers
169	
170	{For each layer you chose, describe:}
171

173	- **Scope**: What gets tested at this layer
174	- **Framework**: {specific tool} — {why this one}
175	- **Location**: `{test directory pattern}` (e.g. `tests/unit/`, `__tests__/`, co-located `*.test.ts`)
176	- **Run command**: `{exact command}` (e.g. `npm run test:unit`, `pytest tests/unit/`)
177	- **Key patterns**:
178	  - {pattern 1, e.g. "Use factories for test data, not raw objects"}
179	  - {pattern 2, e.g. "One assertion per test — name describes the behavior"}
180	
181	## Test Data
182

184	- **Database**: {real DB in Docker / SQLite-in-memory / shared test DB}
185	- **Cleanup**: {transaction rollback / truncate between tests / isolated DB per suite}
186	
187	## CI Integration
188	
189	- **When**: {on every push / on PR / nightly}
190	- **Parallelization**: {split by file / split by layer / single sequential run}
191	- **Required to pass**: {which layers block merge}
192	
193	## What NOT to Test
194	
195	- {e.g. "Third-party library internals — trust the library"}
196	- {e.g. "CSS styling — use visual review, not snapshot tests"}
197	- {e.g. "Generated code — test the generator, not the output"}
198	```
199	
200	### Artifact 2: `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/architecture-decisions.md`
201	
202	```markdown

204	
205	## Patterns
206	
207	{For each pattern you chose, describe:}
208

211	- **Structure**:
212	```{lang}
213	{3-8 line code skeleton showing the pattern}
214	```
215	- **Example file**: `{path}` (if one exists in the repo already)
216	- **Rationale**: {one sentence why}
217	
218	## File Organization
219	
220	```
221	{proposed directory tree for the new feature, 10-15 lines max}
222	```
223	
224	## Conventions
225	
226	| Area | Convention |
227	|------|-----------|
228	| File naming | {e.g. kebab-case for files, PascalCase for components} |
229	| Exports | {e.g. named exports, no default exports} |
230	| Error handling | {e.g. typed errors with codes, no generic catch} |
231	| Config access | {e.g. injected config object, no process.env in business logic} |
232	| Logging | {e.g. structured JSON logs via pino/structlog} |
233	
234	## Dependencies to Add

239	
240	## Decisions NOT Made
241	
242	{List things you deliberately left open for the planner/coder to decide,
243	and why. E.g. "ORM choice deferred — need to see query complexity in tasks"}
244	```

252	| Next.js | `npx create-next-app@latest . --ts --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --no-git --yes` |
253	| Vite (React) | `npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts` |
254	| Nuxt | `npx nuxi init . --no-git` |
255	| SvelteKit | `npx sv create . --template minimal --types ts` |
256	| Remix | `npx create-remix@latest . --no-git-init` |
257	| Django | `django-admin startproject config .` |
258	| FastAPI | manual (no official scaffolder — document minimal file structure) |
259	| Rails | `rails new . --skip-git` |
260	| Go | `go mod init {module}` |
261	| Rust | `cargo init .` |
262	| Flutter | `flutter create .` |
